location: Not a world pickup — dropped as the Remembrance of a God and a Lord by Promised Consort Radahn, the DLC's final boss, atop Enir-Ilim, then traded to Finger Reader Enia at Roundtable Hold. Enia offers one reward per remembrance copy: Greatsword of Radahn (Lord), Greatsword of Radahn (Light), or the Light of Miquella incantation — getting more than one in the same playthrough requires duplicating the remembrance at a Remembrance Duplication Coffin in the Realm of Shadow first.

source.name: Promised Consort Radahn (remembrance, redeemed at Roundtable Hold)

tactics: Promised Consort Radahn's fog gate is directly up the stairs from the Divine Gate Front Staircase site of grace, near the top of Enir-Ilim

This is the DLC's mandatory final boss — a brutal two-phase fight combining Radahn's gravity-and-blade combos with Miquella's holy magic in phase two, so stack Scadutree Fragments before attempting it
At Enia, pick Greatsword of Radahn (Light) if you want the Lightspeed Slash skill's chargeable ranged Holy-beam follow-up rather than the (Lord) variant's pure melee combo
Defeating the boss also unlocks the Gate of Divinity grace just past the arena
lore: A pair of black-steel, lion-maned greatswords Radahn carried in his youth, before he ever leaned on gravity sorcery — proof his monstrous prowess began as pure swordsmanship. This copy is charged with a shard of Miquella's light, letting its wielder move faster than sight for an instant.

builds.name: Str/Dex/Int hybrid caster
builds.why: D/D/E scaling across Strength, Dexterity, and Intelligence; Lightspeed Slash's charged follow-up beams scale off Intelligence and deal Holy damage, rewarding INT investment beyond the bare minimum
